rationale: Y is stronger overall because it uses the Europace-style structured format, states a specific and testable gap, integrates design and results efficiently, and keeps the access implication out of the conclusion. Both abstracts slightly overinterpret an observational IPTW study by implying comorbidities drive the prognosis, since residual confounding cannot be excluded. X overreaches further by suggesting persistent AF alone should not affect ablation access, and it has a clarity problem with the Charlson score phrasing.
loser_weakness: X most lacked certainty calibration. Its conclusion moves from adjusted similarity in outcomes to a stronger causal and policy implication than the retrospective administrative-data design can support.
